params.text:
Low mood or depression?

Everyone feels down, sad or fed up from time to time. This is a normal part of life, and low mood usually lifts within a few days once the cause passes or circumstances change. Depression is different. It is a lasting low mood that goes on for weeks or months and starts to affect how a person thinks, feels and manages everyday life.

  • Low mood: a normal reaction that usually lifts within days.
  • Depression: lasts weeks or longer and affects daily life.
  • The difference: how long it lasts and how much it affects the person.

params.alt: Illustration for Low mood or depression

params.text:
A common and treatable illness

Depression is one of the most common mental health problems, and it can affect anyone, of any age or background. Some people have one episode; others experience depression more than once during their lives. It ranges from mild, where a person can still get through the day with effort, to severe, where even simple tasks can feel impossible.

The most important message is that depression is treatable. With the right support — which may include talking therapies, medication, or both — most people get better. Recognising that someone may be depressed, and helping them reach their GP or mental health services, is one of the most valuable things you can do.

params.question: What is the main difference between everyday low mood and depression?

answers.text: Depression is a lasting low mood, over weeks or months, that affects how a person thinks, feels and copes with daily life.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Correct. It is the length of time and the effect on daily life that mark depression out from ordinary low mood.

answers.text: Low mood only happens to older people, while depression only affects younger people.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Both low mood and depression can affect people of any age; the difference is how long it lasts and its impact.

answers.text: Depression always has an obvious cause, whereas low mood never does.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Depression sometimes has no clear cause at all; the key difference is how lasting it is and how it affects daily life.

answers.text: There is no real difference; the two words mean exactly the same thing.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: They are different. Low mood usually passes in days, while depression lasts and affects everyday life.
